_meta.code: National Building Code of India, Part 4 — Fire and Life Safety
_meta.source_edition: NBC 2005 (this is the edition of the uploaded PDF). NOT NBC 2016.
_meta.target_edition: Project target is NBC 2016 — see edition_warning before using numeric ratings.
covers_checks: D25 — construction-type classification (occupancy group + Type 1/2/3/4 by fire resistance)
D23 — fire-resistance closure (required rating per element vs IS 456 Fig 1 / Table 16A supply)
_meta.encoding_note: Numeric tables transcribed verbatim from the uploaded 2005 PDF (facts the checker reads). Prose rules stated concisely, not reproduced verbatim. Same style as file_for_DBR.json / is1893_part1_2016_clauses.json / is875_part3_2015_clauses.json.
_meta.edition_warning: The uploaded PDF is NBC 2005, not 2016. NBC 2016 Part 4 differs materially for these checks: (1) Table 1 fire-resistance ratings are in MINUTES in 2016 vs HOURS here; (2) several Table 1 rows are restructured/renumbered in 2016 (e.g. 2016 merges 'shafts for services, lift hoistway and refuse chutes' into one row and has no separate 'fire resisting walls' row); (3) some numeric values differ; (4) NBC 2016 expanded occupancy sub-divisions. The Type 1–4 concept and the Groups A–J scheme are otherwise unchanged. Treat the Table 1 values in this file as a LABELLED STOPGAP / cross-reference for the 2005 edition. For an authoritative 2016 corpus, supply the NBC 2016 Part 4 PDF (or readable renders of its Table 1 + Clause 3).

edition_differences_2016_INDICATIVE_ONLY.confidence: low — from secondary OCR sources, NOT the BIS PDF. Do not wire into the checker; for awareness only.
edition_differences_2016_INDICATIVE_ONLY.nbc2016_table1_title: Table 1 Fire Resistance Ratings of Structural and Non-Structural Elements (MINUTES), cited under Cl 3.3.1 and 3.3.2
edition_differences_2016_INDICATIVE_ONLY.nbc2016_rc_critical_rows_status: NOT VERIFIED — interior bearing walls/columns/girders, floor construction and roof construction rows (the ones D23 actually uses for an RC tower) were not legible in the secondary source. MUST come from the 2016 PDF.

numbering_map.nbc2016_per_BIS: Fire-resistance ratings = Table 1 under Cl 3.3.1/3.3.2 (Table 1, NOT 'Table 2'); occupancy = Cl 3.1.
numbering_map.methodology_anchor_discrepancy: Methodology cited 'Table 2 / Cl 3 & 4'. The actual NBC (both editions) puts these in Table 1 / Cl 3.1 + 3.3. Re-check the methodology label against your 2016 copy.

not_in_this_upload: IS 875 (Part 5) temperature — D20 (Cl 3 Temperature Effects + Fig 1 max-temp isopleths + Fig 2 min-temp isopleths) and D14 (load-combination clause, cl. 8). No IS 875-5 file was attached.

clauses.title: High Rise Building
clauses.statement: Any building 15 m or above in height is a high rise building. (Triggers high-rise fire-protection requirements in Annex C and pushes construction towards Type 1/Type 2.)

clauses.title: Classification of Buildings by Occupancy (Groups A–J) and Sub-divisions
clauses.statement: Every building is classified by use into one of nine occupancy groups; each group is further sub-divided. Sub-division drives the fire-protection and exit requirements. For D25, the building's occupancy group + sub-division is the first input; construction Type (1–4) is the second.

subdivisions.label: Lodging or rooming houses
subdivisions.threshold: sleeping accommodation total not more than 40 persons (beds); inns, clubs, motels, guest houses

subdivisions.label: One or two family private dwellings
Dormitories
subdivisions.threshold: group sleeping, non-family; school/college hostels, barracks

subdivisions.label: Schools up to senior secondary level
Theatrical/motion picture/stage, fixed seats up to 1 000 persons
No permanent stage, ≥ 300 persons, no permanent seating
No permanent stage, < 300 persons, no permanent seating
All other (incl. temporary) assembly not in D-1..D-4, at ground level
Other elevated/underground assembly not in D-1..D-6 (e.g. underground/elevated railways)
Laboratories, research establishments, libraries, test houses
Storage / sheltering of goods, ware, merchandise (warehouses, cold storage, depots, garages, hangars, grain elevators, stables, etc.)
Storage/handling/manufacture/processing of highly combustible or explosive materials, or those producing poisonous fumes/explosions

clauses.title: Maximum height ceilings by occupancy (applicability bounds)
clauses.statement: These occupancies are not permitted above the stated height. Relevant to D25 applicability (a claimed occupancy+height combination may be disallowed outright).

clauses.title: Restrictions on Type of Construction for New Buildings (by Fire Zone)
clauses.statement: Permitted construction types depend on the fire zone in which the building sits. Use with the occupancy→fire-zone mapping (Cl 3.2.2.2) to bound the allowed construction Type for D25.

clauses.title: Types of Construction — General (Type 1 / 2 / 3 / 4)
clauses.statement: Construction is classified by fire resistance into four categories: Type 1, Type 2, Type 3, Type 4. Required fire-resistance ratings for structural and non-structural members are given in Table 1 (broadly Type 1 ≈ 4 h, Type 2 ≈ 2 h, Type 3 ≈ 1–2 h, Type 4 ≈ 1 h for primary structural rows). For buildings 15 m in height or above, non-combustible materials should be used and the internal walls of staircase enclosures should be brick/RCC or other material with a minimum 2 h rating; chimney walls shall be Type 1/Type 2 depending on whether flue-gas temperature is above 200°C or not. NOTE: ratings here are in HOURS (2005 edition); NBC 2016 expresses them in minutes.

clauses.title: Requirement — element must have requisite fire-resistance rating
clauses.statement: Each structural/non-structural element/component shall have the fire-resistance rating required by Table 1 when tested per the accepted standard. Fire-damage and post-fire structural-safety assessment may also use Tables 2–18. This is the demand side of D23: required rating (Table 1) ≤ achievable rating from member size + cover (IS 456 Fig 1 / Table 16A).

clauses.title: Steel construction — protection against collapse
clauses.statement: Load-bearing steel beams and columns of buildings with total covered area 500 m² and above shall be protected against failure/collapse in fire, using suitable fire-resistance-rated materials with a suppression system (see Tables 14, 15 and accepted standard). Applies to steel members; for RC the Table 1 ratings + IS 456 cover govern.

clauses.title: Table 1 — Fire Resistance Ratings of Structural and Non-Structural Elements (HOURS)
clauses.statement: Required fire-resistance rating (hours) of each element for each construction Type. 0 = no rating required. 'At least 1/2 h' = 0.5 h minimum. This is the core D23 demand table and the D25 Type-classification basis. NOTE: 2005 edition, HOURS — NBC 2016 uses minutes and a restructured row list.

rows.element: Exterior walls — fire separation < 3.7 m — Bearing
Exterior walls — fire separation < 3.7 m — Non-bearing
Exterior walls — fire separation 3.7 m to < 9 m — Bearing
Exterior walls — fire separation 3.7 m to < 9 m — Non-bearing
Exterior walls — fire separation ≥ 9 m — Bearing
Exterior walls — fire separation ≥ 9 m — Non-bearing
Fire resisting walls
Fire separation assemblies (like fire check doors)
Fire enclosures of exitways, hallways and stairways
Shaft other than exitways, elevator and hoistways
Exitway access corridors
Vertical separation of tenant spaces
Dwelling unit separation
Non-load bearing partitions
rows.note: table states 'At least half an hour' for all types

rows.element: Interior bearing walls, bearing partitions, columns, girders, trusses (other than roof trusses) and framing — Supporting more than one floor
Interior bearing walls, columns, girders, trusses and framing — Supporting one floor only
Interior bearing walls, columns, girders, trusses and framing — Supporting a roof only
Structural members supporting walls
Floor construction including walls
Roof construction — 5 m or less in height to lowest member
Roof construction — more than 5 m but less than 6.7 m to lowest member
Roof construction — 6.7 m or more in height to lowest member

clauses.title: Table 19 — Comparative Floor Area Ratios by Occupancy and Construction Type (street ≥ 9 m wide)
clauses.statement: Primarily a FAR-limit table, but the entries also indicate which construction Types are permitted for each occupancy: a number/UL = permitted, NP = that Type is not permitted for that occupancy. Secondary context for D25 (e.g. Hazardous cannot be Type 4). Individual municipal corporations may alter the values but must keep the ratios between occupancies/types.
